etymology.description: 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + 荅(荅) (dá, "reply") = semantic element indicating structure + phonetic element -> tower; pagoda
etymology.interpretation: The character 塔 combines 土(土) (tǔ, "earth"), which indicates a structure built on earth, with 荅(荅) (dá, "reply") for its phonetic component. This suggests a tall structure like a tower or pagoda, typically founded on the earth.

examples.chinese: 这座旧塔已有几百年的历史(這座舊塔已有幾百年的歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò jiù tǎ yǐ yǒu jǐ bǎi nián de lìshǐ.
examples.english: This old tower has a history of several hundred years.

examples.chinese: 在山顶上可以看到远处的塔(在山頂上可以看到遠處的塔)。
examples.pinyin: Zài shāndǐng shàng kěyǐ kàn dào yuǎn chù de tǎ.
examples.english: From the mountain top, you can see the tower in the distance.

examples.chinese: 这座城市因其标志性的塔而闻名(這座城市因其標誌性的塔而聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò chéngshì yīn qí biāozhì xìng de tǎ ér wénmíng.
examples.english: This city is famous for its iconic tower.
